title: Enable/disable weekend selection at column level
excerpt: I have different boards that need different controls for weekends. But your timeline weekend control is overarching which means we have either boards that wont include weekends when we need it, or boards that do include …

posts.author: brittanywildridge (Brittany)
posts.cookedHtml: I have different boards that need different controls for weekends. But your timeline weekend control is overarching which means we have either boards that wont include weekends when we need it, or boards that do include weekends when we don’t need it. Please give boards the power to control timeline weekends

posts.author: IvanRivera (Ivan Rivera)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does anyone else need to see timeline weekends in some boards but also need to omit them in other boards?

I think this could be a good feature to add

Ivan

posts.author: fredlierman (Fred Lierman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this would be a great feature.
Overall custom workdays per board would be great.

For example, my organisation gives employees every other Friday off additional to the weekends.

posts.author: ynguyen (Yun)
posts.cookedHtml: This would absolutely benefit us!

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone

You can now do all these using the Date Check app. Using templates, you can set weekends or non-working days for individual boards.
Click here for early access.

posts.author: marcy (Marcy M.)
posts.cookedHtml:
The weekends setting should be a little more flexible, either by board or workspace. Previously, it only applied to timeline columns and it was recently (Nov 22) retro-fitted for date columns as well. This has wreaked havoc on my organization as we sometimes need to set weekends as a running date for an item but the deadline should always be on the weekday (we don’t work weekends). This has caused a lot of issues for us when using monday.com to create timelines with dependencies as we now have to manually update each line item to not include weekends. Help please!!!

It would be great to also be able to set holidays (company closures) by board or workspace alongside the ability to choose weekends or not.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Marcy,

Just out of interest, why would you need to plan a deadline in a weekend? Is that because monday dependency does not work correctly with deadlines (that should really take 0 days from a planning perspective).

Our partner Work Perfect has an app SchedulerPro that support real deadlines (taking up 0 days). The app is in the process of getting listed at the monday marketplace. You can express your interest by using this form here . The contact person for this amazing app is @Kimb

Very soon after initial release the holiday blackout will be introduced. This will work as a view where you can create multiple schedule. Each different board can use a different schedule. Below is a sneak preview of the working day schedules view. The app also supports lead and lag days and all 4 dependency types.

image1122×1119 73.3 KB

posts.author: marcy (Marcy M.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this. I went ahead and filled out the form. This will be perfect for my timeline boards and I have been asking about holiday blackouts since we acquired our monday.com account.

To clarify, we’re an advertising agency so our traffic team will set weekday deadlines based on (sometimes) weekend run dates of a publication. However, during our busy season, we will sometimes even have to set deadlines over the weekend. The ability to be flexible with working days from board to board is extremely helpful.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there! We wanted to let you know that we will be merging this topic into this more recently posted matching feature request -Control timeline weekends per board - in order to consolidate matching threads and ensure votes are accurate and up-to-date. If you did not cast a vote on this topic, please add your vote to the active thread above. Thanks for your understanding

posts.author: sswale (Sandra Swale)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree, I need to skip weekends on some projects, on others I need weekends. I need the ability to manage this at the board level, not the admin level

posts.author: cdf (Carrie d.)
posts.cookedHtml: We have one team that holds events on weekends, and needs the ability to select a weekend date in the timeline/date column. While I know I can enable/disable weekends for our entire instance across the platform, it would be helpful to enable this on a board-by-board basis, so this one particular team can see their weekend events on their timelines/calendars/dashboards, but we dont have to toggle the weekend view off for every other team across the platform.

posts.author: lucidmattnj (Matt D)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is there any way to shut off the ability to use weekends at the board level, rather than just the account level, when using a timeline? On the whole I don’t want timelines to reflect weekend days but there are circumstances where that is avoidable. However I want it to be be limited to just the boards that need to infringed on weekends, not every timeline that is generated.

Separate but related, is there a way to define non-working days at either the account or the board level (akin to SmartSheets)? So if I wanted to block out 7/4, 12/25, 1/1 from being reflected in timelines, do I need to set those as specific items in a board with a dependency to move around (which is clunky) or can I just eliminate them as working days?

posts.author: sbarrington (Stuart Barrington)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had a call today with our TAM and had similar questions. I was told today that:

  • Workdays is a global setting so it will impact all Boards in all Workspaces
  • Adding in specific days (i.e. National Holidays) is a WIP.

posts.author: alex.walton (Alex Walton)
posts.cookedHtml: Commenting here to say that our team is very large and we utilize workspaces. Some workspaces need weekends available for work and some need to block them to fully utilize dependencies to adjust timelines. It would be great to allow for the weekends to be blocked at the workspace level at the very least to allow for different teams to work the way they need to in a large enterprise.

posts.author: marcy (Marcy M.)
posts.cookedHtml: Just here to add that this would also be helpful for my organization! Hoping Monday.com issues a fix for this.

posts.author: Backup7074 (Tristan)
posts.cookedHtml: I didn’t even realize this wasn’t default behavior – now that somebody with admin access to Monday has set this option though it is an extreme issue. I am not using my board for deadlines, I am using it to keep records of things happening and need to accurately select dates! It would be much appreciated if this could be corrected.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone

You can now enable weekend date selection by board basis using the Date Check app.

Click here for early access

posts.author: mniswonger (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on being able to turn off weekends and holidays at a board level? I turned it off globally but it impacts everyone using Monday.com.

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @mniswonger @lucidmattnj @sbarrington ,

Check out the Date Check which we will be releasing soon. You can do exactly this and more.
Click here for early access.

Here is a demo

posts.author: KLS13 (Kirsty)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

It would be great if a feature was introduced to set custom weeks/weekends at a board level so teams can better leverage using the dependency and duration/timeline.

At present the only way to ‘turn weekends off’ is at an account level which is not always appropriate as different teams/departments may have different working weeks Mon-Sat, Sat-Thur, Mon-Fri.

Allowing custom weeks would benefit users worldwide.

posts.author: around (Allison Round)
posts.cookedHtml: My team would also would benefit from this feature. Weekends are required for some of our boards, but not all. Add the ability to include/exclude weekends in the Board Settings.

posts.author: Fabrice (DELANNOY)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
A few monday users need to schedule actions on Saturday and Sunday. The display of weekends is general parameter.
I would like a possible parameter on each board.
Thank you,
Fabrice.

posts.author: brooks (Brooks Williams)
posts.cookedHtml: I just wanted to voice my support for moving this setting to the Workspace (or even board) level. We have some users that need to track expirations and sometimes those happen over the weekend. To allow this we have to globally enable weekends, which is not ideal at all.

posts.author: brittanywildridge (Brittany)
posts.cookedHtml: Fully agree. We need both types for different seasons of work but our seasons overlap for months at a time and it becomes very challenging for scheduling. Please make the option available per board

posts.author: cmohrmann (Christine Mohrmann)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an old post, but is there any update to changing permissions to the workspace level to include or hide weekends? Thanks.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone,

I have reached out to the product team in-charge for an update on this - I will get back to you all as soon as I have information to share. Thank you for your continued patience

Update: At this time, due to some feature prioritisation changes, we don’t currently have an update on the development of this feature which I understand isn’t ideal for you all however please know the team are aware of the need and demand for this release, so it is on their radar however no ETA yet

posts.author: stephITPM (Stephanie)
posts.cookedHtml:
How exactly does one enable weekends at the board level once receiving early access to the Date Check app? I watched the 13 min demo and may have missed something, but from what I understand we would have to set weekends as if they are holidays for the boards where we don’t want weekends to be an option, by creating an automation?

based on the level of work that needs to go into creating this option per board, what setting do you recommend to be account-wide if the majority of boards should not have weekends as an option?

We are looking for a way to leave our account-wide settings to no weekends, and enable weekends for specific boards based on the board and not the person assigned. Some people will do work on weekends for certain types of projects but should not be able to be assigned on a weekend for other projects.

Also, is the Date Check app free and going to remain free once it’s released?

And is there an update from Monday.com about whether a feature like this is being considered without needing to do all the work to configure this via a separate app and training our users who need it to also do that?

posts.author: jacsene (Jacqueline Sene)
posts.cookedHtml: This is really important at the enterprise level with several teams using Monday and having different needs at the board level.

posts.author: bob1 (bob)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes please, at the very least make it a workspace setting instead of an account setting.

posts.author: torrie (Torrie)
posts.cookedHtml:
I read on another board that Holiday & Blackout dates were added to the long-term roadmap. Would board / workspace working days / weekends be included in that exploration?

It is critical for some boards to allow / include weekends and for others to be weekdays only.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Checking on this for you Torrie!

posts.author: gillian_nation (Gillian Nation)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

This feature is imperative to our success working with Monday. I’ve escalated with various product teams at monday but would like a timeline estimate on this feature. Bianca can you tell us when this is coming?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Torrie and Gillian,

I am still waiting to hear more concrete information as the development of this feature is dependent on additional developments in other domains… I am doing my best to understand specifics asap!

posts.author: QuinnC (Quinn Colter)
posts.cookedHtml: This seems like quite a basic feature requirement for work management software; I’m surprised it’s not already available.

posts.author: seanmcc (Sean McClintock)
posts.cookedHtml: Just to put yet another fine point on the conversation – I struggle to see how the ability to assign a unique calendar to each board is not a part of the basic functionality. Especially in a large organization you have hundreds of teams working across different shifts and timezones. Even within a single development team you may have teams in one TZ working on a given unit and a team in a distinct time zone working on another. It’s critical that we have the ability to set the calendar at the board level.

posts.author: BetsyO (Betsy Orton)
posts.cookedHtml: Ability to add or remove weekends is critical as an enterprise level software. We need weekends for our deployment team, however my team needs to exclude them. Now each of our dates are of by 0-2 days, but when you multiply that by 50+ sub items - we’re now off by 100 days/3 months! Please don’t make us do each timeline manually or buy an extension app that you don’t control and adds additional cost!

posts.author: Cotters (Martin Cottingham)
posts.cookedHtml:
To be honest, this is a major flaw for a timeline based plan. Without control of when the actual work days are it is impossible to correctly schedule anything with dependent tasks, because if a preceding task moves it can and does push things into the weekend that can’t be done at the weekend.
This requires control at the board level because not all plans will be the same, we also want SOME weekends to be used, ie: for a major deployment, but not all.
I have a plan with 15 product launches and it is proving really hard to control if one or two of the earlier tasks move, because they are all dependent.
Otherwise, I do like Monday.

posts.author: jb303 (Jessica)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that this is a major weakness for a project planning system. There are times my team needs to set deadlines or effective dates on a weekend but turning it on for the entire organization has a detrimental impact on other teams because it impacts their durations and timelines. This should be able to be controlled at the board level.

posts.author: bradhasha (Brad Hasha)
posts.cookedHtml: @BiancaT Another example of a product flaw. How is this not capable of being altered anywhere but globally? I have infrastructure folks who have weekend infra windows, but I don’t want weekend factored into timeline planning for projects. Very disappointed with yet another gotcha.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Brad,

I appreciate that you’re frustrated and recognise that there is a community demand for this feature.

I am working with teams internally to better understand the reasoning behind delays in development. At this stage I want to let you know that this particular feature - controlling timeline weekends per board/workspace - is on our list for development this year (likely H2 however subject to change). For some context, we know that our time capabilities need improving and are currently focusing on these areas within the platform, prioritising quality and stability which takes time to ensure the build is right.

Please be assured we are advocating for our community each and every day.

posts.author: GabrielMaglia (Gabriel Maglia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca!
Could you specify when in H2 is this feature expected to go live?
This is a serious issue that my company needs to get fixed.
Having teams working on weekends and other teams only working from Monday to Friday we had to choose our Week Days to have weekends included. But this has totally disabled any way for us to use the timeline columns and gantt graphs for planning out projects.

On top of that, how would having 2 different accounts look like as a quick fix? But obviously paying for one, as we deeply need to have timelines to behave differently on different areas of the company.

Thanks!

posts.author: Cari (Cari A.)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very beneficial for our organization as well. We have some teams that travel to events and tradeshows on the weekends which need to be captured for their schedules, but in other boards tracking tasks we don’t want to assign work on the weekends. After 3 years it appears of many people asking for this feature I hope this is truly coming in the near future.

posts.author: YKarim (Yussef)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca

When can we expect an update?

Its a basic, but very important part of project planning.

posts.author: KrissyLee16 (Krissy)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, this would be great for us too. The problem I have is with the calendar view (and I’m having a REALLY hard time getting either Google or Outlook calendars to link up, much less sync). If I set workdays to Monday-Friday, turn off weekends (or sometimes even when turned on), and choose the day I want the work week and calendar to display, I either get the wrong first day, the right first day but it stops at Thursday, a 5-day week that starts on Sunday and cuts off Friday, or a Monday-Friday week that ignores Monday and Friday as “weekend”. I finally gave up, but if anyone has a solution to that…

posts.author: monomono (Moe Nomura)
posts.cookedHtml:
When using the “Hide Weekends” calendar function, Friday and Saturday are hidden.
We have Saturdays and Sundays as holidays and would like to be able to choose to hide those days as well.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @monomono,

I believe this is an account-wide setting that is set on your behalf by your monday workspace administrator.

image720×319 22.7 KB

Hopefully that is a quick change for you, or perhaps you have a lot of colleagues that observe weekend on Friday and Saturday, and changing it is not an option. In that case you can also have a look at the free Calendar Plus app in the marketplace. You can configure a new calendar view that specifically hides Saturday and Sunday.

posts.author: KathyD (Kathryn Davidson)
posts.cookedHtml: My Organization uses M-F work week. Some teams work 7-day M-Su work weeks. We can’t even select a weekend day in a calendar column of any boards, which means our weekend working teams can’t use Monday at all. Make it so calendars can select a weekend day or make it so certain teams or boards can enable 7-day work week, without messing up the entire organisation’s other M-F week team functions.

posts.author: gvelasco1493 (Gerardo Velasco Aguiar )
posts.cookedHtml:
There has been a need in large companies to be able to configure the dashboard to enable weekends or disable them.

It is difficult when more than 200-500 users use Mondays to establish that everyone works on weekends or not. In many cases it is varied.

I think this option is necessary to stay in large accounts and venture into new ones.

posts.author: arf (Alex R-F)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wholeheartedly agree that this is crucial functionality for any tool used by a large and/or distributed organization.

Just FYI: there’s an existing feature request thread about this here; I’d encourage you to upvote it too. I wish I had more faith that those votes and comments would actually result in the necessary action, but it’s worth a try.

posts.author: gvelasco1493 (Gerardo Velasco Aguiar )
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you Alex, I see that many people have the same need, I hope someone can do something!
Regards

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi everyone, I am happy to share that this feature is currently in Alpha and will be released to all accounts shortly! We appreciate all the feedback around this feature and helping us bring it to life. Your ideas and thoughts have been really important as our team designs and develop these features. Keep voting and sharing your ideas!

posts.author: Benkazinik (Ben Kazinik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Want to automate your project timeline? Here’s a great post we did on the best way to create a project timeline

monday-project-timeline1600×834 161 KB

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

Amber here from the team responsible for our date and timeline columns.

I’m happy to share that we have released the ability to enable or disable weekends at the column level via the column settings:

image818×612 35.2 KB

The default behaviour of these columns will still go according to the account settings as defined in the Admin settings, but you will be able to override these settings at any time with the above toggling in the column!

Enjoy!! If you have any feedback, we’d love to hear it

posts.author: kmunger (Katye Munger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Amber!

This is great for a Date Column, but I don’t see it available as a setting in the Timeline Column. Am I missing something? (Honestly, I can’t see it as an option in my Date Column, either - if my board was created before this was released would that mean the settings aren’t there?)
